workflow.name: Summarize daily Jenkins test runs with Google Sheets, HTTP and Gemini AI
workflow.description:
## Automate daily Jenkins test reports with AI and HTTP Requests
As a test automation engineer, staying on top of daily test runs in Jenkins is essential. This workflow automates that process: it pulls specific test details from a Google Sheet, retrieves data from your local Jenkins environment, and uses AI to generate a concise summary report to be sent via email.

**Who's it for**
* Test automation engineers using Jenkins.
* QA teams looking to streamline daily reporting.

**How it works** 
* Scheduled Trigger: The workflow runs automatically at a pre-defined time.
* Dynamic Data Retrieval: It constructs an HTTP request based on the data in your Google Sheet to fetch specific Jenkins results.
* AI Optimization: Only relevant data is extracted to minimize AI token usage and focus on the most important metrics.
* Summarization: The AI groups the results and formats them into a clear, professional email.
* Distribution: The report is sent to every recipient listed in the MailingList column.


**How to set up** 
* In the Google Sheet, set the BaseUrl, Environment, FeatureClass and Feature in order to build up the Jenkins url in their corresponding columns, for example:
BaseUrl	   | Environment	|  FeatureClass	      |Feature |MailingList |
<BaseUrl> |<environment>	| <FeaturClassName> |<Featurename>  |	<mail>     |
* Define Recipients: In the MailingList column, add the email addresses of the people who need to receive the report. If there are multiple recipients, ensure they are separated by commas.


**Requirements** 
* Access to your Jenkins server.
* An AI API key (e.g., Gemini, OpenAI).
* A Google Cloud project with the Google Sheets API enabled.

nodes.name: Aggregate the data for AI processing

parameters.text:
Use the provided data to create an overview of the failed ("Gefaald"), skipped ("Overgeslagen"), passed ("Pass") and total ("Totaal") tests for each feature.
If it has failed ("Gefaald") tests, list them by name.

Summize them per environment and per feature and combine them in one email.

nodes.name: Send the test results based on the MailingList
Perform HTTP Request to Jenkins Server for build numbers
nodes.notes: The first request is needed to have the build numbers available.

parameters.url: ={{ $node["Retrieve Google Sheet data"].json.BaseUrl }}/{{ $node["Retrieve Google Sheet data"].json.Environment }}/lastCompletedBuild/testReport/{{ $node["Retrieve Google Sheet data"].json.FeatureClass }}.{{ $node["Retrieve Google Sheet data"].json.Feature }}/

nodes.name: Set the fields correctly for AI
Perform HTTP Request with depth 2 to Jenkins Server

parameters.url: ={{ $node["Retrieve Google Sheet data"].json.BaseUrl }}/{{ $node["Retrieve Google Sheet data"].json.Environment }}/lastCompletedBuild/testReport/{{ $node["Retrieve Google Sheet data"].json.FeatureClass }}.{{ $node["Retrieve Google Sheet data"].json.Feature }}/api/json?depth=2

nodes.name: Trigger workflow daily on set time

parameters.content:
## Retrieve the Jenkins Test Data
* First we have to retrieve the report in order to get the build numbers, needed for the AI to know which report we are talking about.
* Then we extract the build numbers from the test reports using the HTML extract node.
* In order to have a complete test report, we perform an additional HTTP request but with an "/api/json?depth=2" to get all data, including the failed results.

main.node: Send the test results based on the MailingList
Perform HTTP Request with depth 2 to Jenkins Server
Perform HTTP Request to Jenkins Server for build numbers
Aggregate the data for AI processing
Set the fields correctly for AI
